turns.text: editmore_vertUserYou are analyzing a YouTube video transcript. Extract and structure the following information as valid JSON only (no markdown, no extra text).Return ONLY this JSON structure:{"summary": "2-3 sentence summary of the video content","keyPoints": ["point 1", "point 2", ...],"aiConcepts": ["AI concept 1", "AI concept 2", ...],"technicalDetails": ["tool/framework 1", "implementation detail", ...],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 120, "reason": "Code demo", "context": "Shows Python implementation"}]}If the video is not about AI/tech, set aiConcepts and technicalDetails to empty arrays but still extract keyPoints.TRANSCRIPT:[Music] hi welcome to another video so ever since v0 came out I've always been on the lookout for something that can do the same stuff that v0 can do but locally when bolt came out I was pretty excited to see that it's open source and maybe we could use it locally but I was disappointed when I saw that it only works with anthropic API keys I mean it's good but not everyone can use an expensive anthropic model I don't use it I rely on using free apis like from gemini metral or even local olama models so I was disappointed to say the least but it seems we now have a proper tool that can be used locally and it's actually a fork of bolts open-source version now this is an actual fork or copy of the bolt open- Source repo but this Fork has added some pretty cool features that make it much more usable locally and with multiple providers let's talk a little about it so this is basically bolt but with these features first of all it has open rouer integration Gemini integration and Al model support it can also filter models by provider download projects as zip and includes improved improvements to the system prompt as well as deep seek API integration mistol API integration and open AI compatible API integration it also has the ability to sync files to a local folder it can even publish projects directly to GitHub which is amazing these are the major improvements that it has over the basic bolt you'd host locally let's get it installed and see all this stuff because that will help us understand everything it offers when we see it first of all you'll need to clone it on your computer with this command once you've cloned it you'll need to enter the folder now run this pnpm install command this will install the dependencies once it's installed just rename the EnV example file to EnV local like this now open it up and here you'll need to enter the a keys for the providers you want to use like Gro open AI anthropic deep seek Mistral AMA and your custom open AI base URL I like using it with Gemini and Mistral because both apis are free so I generally get that set up for today I'll mainly focus on using it with the free mrol API because it's free with no rate limit and is super useful for local usage but I'll also show you how you can use it with the GitHub models API as well if you want because I think it'll be pretty good with this anyway once everything is set up here just start it with the pnpm preview command once done it'll start and you'll see the local host and port number where it's running just open it up and you'll see this interface as you can see this interface is a bit different from the general bolt open- source project let's go through it one by one so first of all here you have these providers including for example if I select here you can see that we get all our models to choose from I don't usea for this scale of code generation because it wouldn't work well in this case with a model like 7B so I use Mistral because it works best for me I also have the API key configured for it so let's just select it now once you do that you can see all these options from metrol I like using the large model so let's select that now let's ask it to make a to-do app because I just want to show what it has over the original bolt anyway it's generating now let's wait a bit okay it's done so you can see the code and preview here also if you're wondering how it's showing the code and preview it uses web containers which is basically like a VM inside your browser which is pretty cool so this do doesn't require any API Keys which is amazing anyway here you have the preview and code and everything you can also edit the code here if you want but we have other features here that aren't in the default bolt here you get the download code option sync files option toggle terminal option and push to GitHub option toggle terminal basically opens up the terminal which is great and you can execute any command here if you want apart from this you have the download and sync options the download option basically archives the entire code it generates and lets you download it the sync option allows you to choose a folder and then it will keep syncing the contents of the bolt web container with that folder so you'll always have the updated code there there's also the push to GitHub option which pushes your code to a repo of your choice it's useful though not essential for me this is the main stuff it has over the original one I think it's pretty great to use for free locally with the free apis but let me also show you how you can use it with the GitHub models free API and use it with GPT 40 for free because I think that it could be a really great way to use this this way you can use any model without messing with the constants file ins such so to use it with the GitHub modelsfree API you'll first need to go to GitHub models and get an API Key by clicking get API key and creating one here you can use gp40 for om mini and other models for free with this API you can check my video on it where I go in depth once done you'll need to create a light llm proxy server that will make the GitHub model API I fully open AI compatible to do that install Light llm with this command once done export your GitHub models API key and then start the light llm server with this command now you'll see a URL here just copy this URL go to the bolt EnV file find the open AI like base and enter that URL you get from Light llm which will generally look like this now you can start it and in the interface select the open AI like option here you'll see the model you've configured with light llm so just select that now you can start using it for a demo I'll create a simple mind sweeper game because I don't want to exhaust my rate limits for the day okay it's generating let's wait a bit [Music] and it's done okay this looks pretty good and works well so that's how you can use it with GitHub models free API as well and I think it's just great it gives you the same bolt but with the option to use it with anything I thought this was a pretty good tool to have especially with free apis for me this beats v0 for 100% free usage overall it's pretty cool anyway let let me know your thoughts in the comments if you liked this video consider donating to my Channel Through the super thanks option below or you can also consider becoming a member by clicking the join button also give this video a thumbs up and subscribe to my channel I'll see you in the next video till then bye a [Music] [Music] hi welcome to another video so ever since v0 came out I've always been on the lookout for something that can do the same stuff that v0 can do but locally when bolt came out I was pretty excited to see that it's open source and maybe we could use it locally but I was disappointed when I saw that it only works with anthropic API keys I mean it's good but not everyone can use an expensive anthropic model I don't use it I rely on using free apis like from gemini metral or even local olama models so I was disappointed to say the least but it seems we now have a proper tool that can be used locally and it's actually a with this API you can check my video on it where I go in depth once done you'll need to create a light llm proxy server that will make the GitHub model API I fully open AI compatible to do that install Light llm with this command once done export your GitHub models API key and then start the light llm server with this command now you'll see a URL here just copy this URL go to the bolt EnV file find the open AI like base and enter that URL you get from Light llm which will generally look like this now you can start it and in the interface select the open AI like option here you'll see the model you've configured with light llm so just select that now you can start using it for a demo I'll create a simple mind sweeper game because I don't want to exhaust my rate limits for the day okay it's generating let's wait a bit [Music] and it's done okay this looks pretty good and works well so that's how you can use it with GitHub models free API as well and I think it's just great it gives you the same bolt but with the option to use it with anything I thought this was a pretty good tool to have especially with free apis for me this beats v0 for 100% free usage overall it's pretty cool anyway let let me know your thoughts in the comments if you liked this video consider donating to my Channel Through the super thanks option below or you can also consider becoming a member by clicking the join button also give this video a thumbs up and subscribe to my channel I'll see you in the next video till then bye a [Music]

more_vertModel Thoughts Defining JSON StructureI'm currently focused on defining the JSON structure for the YouTube video data. I've decided on the following keys: summary, keyPoints, aiConcepts, technicalDetails, and visualContextFlags. The goal is to extract and structure information from the transcript into this format; it should be JSON only, no markdown or extra text.Developing Fork FunctionalityI'm now fully immersed in the local fork of Bolt.new. The key is broader LLM support: local models via Ollama, free APIs such as Mistral, Gemini, etc. I've set up syncing to local folders, and a GitHub push functionality. The terminal access feature is working. I'm focusing on the multi-provider aspect. I'm now working on the set-up steps: cloning, installing, and configuring the environment, and running the preview.Integrating LLMs NowI'm making progress. I've integrated support for multiple LLMs, including Ollama, Gemini, Mistral, and DeepSeek, which is a major update. Downloading projects as ZIP files, syncing to local folders, and pushing to GitHub are also functional. The environment is VM-like, using Web Containers. I've demonstrated integration with the GitHub Models API, using LiteLLM to access GPT-4o for free. I'm focusing on those AI concepts, especially the local LLMs, and API integrations.Adding Terminal and DemoI've integrated the GitHub Models API via LiteLLM. A Mind Sweeper game demo is also ready. I'm prioritizing checking JSON validity, and ensuring there are no markdown code blocks or introductory/trailing text, maintaining a clean JSON format. Expand to view model thoughts  chevron_right

editmore_vert{"summary": "This video introduces an enhanced local fork of the Bolt open-source project, providing a free alternative to Vercel's v0 for AI-driven web development. It demonstrates how to set up the tool to work with multiple providers including Ollama, Mistral, and Gemini, while highlighting features like local file syncing and GitHub integration.","keyPoints": ["The original Bolt open-source project was limited to expensive Anthropic API keys.","A new fork of Bolt enables support for Ollama, Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, and OpenRouter.","Key features include downloading projects as ZIP files, syncing files to local folders, and pushing code directly to GitHub.","The tool uses Web Containers to run a virtual environment in the browser for instant code execution and preview.","Users can integrate the GitHub Models free API by using LiteLLM as a proxy server to access GPT-4o."],"aiConcepts": ["Local LLMs","API Integration","OpenAI Compatible APIs","LLM Proxying","Code Generation","System Prompt Optimization"],"technicalDetails": ["pnpm (Package Manager)","Web Containers","LiteLLM","Ollama","Mistral API","GitHub Models API","Environment variables configuration (.env.local)"],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 128, "reason": "Interface overview", "context": "Shows the multi-provider selection menu including Ollama and Mistral"},{"timestamp": 204, "reason": "Code and Preview demo", "context": "Demonstrates the side-by-side code editor and live preview of a generated To-Do app"},{"timestamp": 218, "reason": "Feature showcase", "context": "Displays the download, sync, terminal, and GitHub deployment buttons"},{"timestamp": 354, "reason": "Terminal implementation", "context": "Shows how to set up LiteLLM proxy for GitHub Models integration"},{"timestamp": 420, "reason": "Final demo", "context": "Shows a functional Mind Sweeper game generated using the GPT-4o model"}]}thumb_upthumb_down
